title: rubus parviflorus f. hypomalacus
intro: Rubus parviflorus f. hypomalacus, also known as the western thimbleberry, is a sub-species of the thimbleberry plant, characterized by its small, white flowers and edible red berries. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The health benefits of the western thimbleberry include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. Western thimbleberry is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and can also be consumed fresh or dried as a supplement.
